normative_requirement: Every run selects a versioned target-specific Source Profile and exact target candidate before initialization; source bytes receive SHA-256 identity, immutable descriptors, authority/license/privacy metadata, stable specimen IDs, and an aggregate Source Lock.
story_application: Apply unchanged to the category-neutral synthetic task definition; conversational and Format 02 clauses are inactive conditional branches.

normative_requirement: Each run starts from a configured evidence workspace with an exact target candidate and target-specific source profile.
story_application: Bind the selected synthetic run to the exact repository-owned target-definition candidate and source profile.

normative_requirement: Define required, recommended, optional, and prohibited source roles independently for each compilation target and category profile.
story_application: Load the versioned synthetic-definition Source Profile with one required governed_task_definition role and explicit prohibitions.

normative_requirement: Inspect configured paths, archive readability, source type, expected role, and version hints before committing the run to analysis.
story_application: Run diagnostics before any run event or Source Lock commit and return actionable typed failures.

normative_requirement: Index approved directory and ZIP sources in place while preserving source immutability and recorded hashes.
story_application: Support file, directory, and ZIP inspection without writing, renaming, or extracting into the source location.

normative_requirement: The exact target candidate resolves to a real accessible directory or another target type explicitly supported by its profile.
story_application: The governed profile explicitly supports the repository-relative file candidate and bounded directory/ZIP test variants.

normative_requirement: Record source identifiers, canonical paths, cryptographic hashes, sizes, timestamps where available, roles, precedence, and authority status in an immutable Source Lock.
story_application: Create stable descriptors and a deterministic aggregate Source Lock; changed bytes create a new version and invalidation evidence.

normative_requirement: Source profiles use resolvable URIs or paths and support local directories and archives without one machine-specific layout.
story_application: Persist repository-relative repo:// identities, never an absolute workstation path, for the governed proof.

normative_requirement: Configured source repositories, archives, and specimens are read-only evidence unless an explicit governed import or migration creates a new copy.
story_application: Open inputs read-only, compare pre/post bytes and metadata in tests, and write only to in-memory bounded Story state.

normative_requirement: Prevent archive traversal, unsafe extraction, executable surprise, and unbounded recursive ingestion.
story_application: Reject traversal, absolute or duplicate members, symlinks, executables, nested archives, ratio/budget overflow, and excessive recursion/count.
